heading: Recovery of excess rifles, ammunition, and parts granted to foreign countries and transfer to certain persons
label: 40728B Recovery of excess rifles, ammunition, and parts granted to foreign countries and transfer to certain persons

Report on Acquisition and Transfer, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–328, div. A, title X, § 1096(d)130 Stat. 2441Dec. 23, 2016“(1).—Report requiredNot later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [], the Secretary of the Army shall submit to the Committee on Armed Services and the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate and the Committee on Armed Services and the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives a report on the acquisition and transfer of excess rifles, ammunition, repair parts, and other supplies described in , United States Code, that were provided to a country on a grant basis under the conditions imposed by section 505 of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 []. The report shall include each of the following:Dec. 23, 2016section 40731(a) of title 3622 U.S.C. 2314“(A)A list of excess rifles, ammunition, repair parts, and other supplies known to the United States Army as eligible for transfer under , United States Code.section 40731(a) of title 36“(B)An assessment of whether and how the Secretary of the Army intends to use the authorities under , United States Code, as added by this section.section 40728B of title 36“(C)Any other issue that the Secretary of the Army considers appropriate.“(2).—Prohibition on transfers pending submittal of reportNo rifle, ammunition, repair part, or supplies acquired under , United States Code, may be transferred until the date that is 90 days after the date of the submittal of the report required under paragraph (1).”section 40728B(a) of title 36

children.label: A
children.text: provided to any country on a grant basis under the conditions imposed by section 505 of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 () that became excess to the needs of such country; and22 U.S.C. 2314
The Secretary of the Army may not acquire anything under paragraph (1) except for transfer to a person in the United States under subsection (c).
The Secretary of the Army may accept rifles, ammunition, repair parts, or other supplies under paragraph (1) notwithstanding .section 1342 of title 31

children.heading: .—Cost of Recovery
children.label: b .—Cost of Recovery
children.text: The Secretary of the Army may not acquire anything under subsection (a) if the United States would incur any cost for such acquisition.
is licensed as a manufacturer, importer, or dealer pursuant to ; andsection 923(a) of title 18
uses an ammunition depot of the Army that is an eligible facility for receipt of any rifles, ammunition, repair parts, or supplies under this paragraph.

children.heading: .—Market Value
children.label: d .—Market Value
children.text: The Secretary of the Army may only transfer an item under subsection (c) if the Secretary receives fair market value for the item.

children.heading: .—Contracts
children.label: e .—Contracts
children.text: Notwithstanding , the Secretary may enter into such contracts or cooperative agreements on a sole source basis pursuant to paragraphs (4) and (5) of section 3204(a) to carry out this section.section 3201(e) of title 101So in original. Probably means .1section 3204(a) of title 10

children.heading: .—AECA
children.label: f .—AECA
children.text: Transfers authorized under this section may only be made in accordance with applicable provisions of the Arms Export Control Act ().22 U.S.C. 2778

children.heading: .—Rifle Defined
children.label: g .—Rifle Defined
children.text: In this section, the term “rifle” has the meaning given such term in .section 921 of title 18
